name: Mayotte (France)

colors.symbolism: the central panel bearing the territorial arms
the blue of the central shield, recalling the surrounding Indian Ocean and the lagoon of Mayotte
the red elements of the arms
the gold details of the arms — the moon, sun, and ylang-ylang flowers

description: A vertical tricolour of green, white and green with a wider central white stripe charged with the territorial coat of arms — a blue shield supported by two white seahorses, with a motto scroll below.
history: The official flag of Mayotte is the French Tricolore; this local flag, based on the territorial coat of arms granted in 1982, is widely used as a regional emblem. Mayotte separated from the rest of the Comoros at independence in 1975, choosing to remain French; it became an Overseas Department (DOM) on 31 March 2011 and is part of the European Union as an outermost region.

features.symbolism: green stripes flanking a wide white central panel that bears the territorial coat of arms

the territorial arms feature a blue shield with a horizontal white wavy band (the lagoon) over a crescent (recalling the Comorian Sultanate that historically ruled Mayotte) and an ylang-ylang flower (Mayotte's chief export); two seahorses serve as supporters, with the motto 'RA HACHIRI' ('we are vigilant') on a ribbon below
